reason: Load the backend bootstrap contract before creating the first backend code.
Use the agreed first-pass backend layout when scaffolding packages and files.
Implement SQLite, Redis cache hydration, and config persistence consistently.
Follow the backend error contract for upload, admin, and health endpoints.
Apply the logging and redaction expectations from the first pass.
Keep tests and implementation boundaries aligned with backend quality expectations.
Load the frontend bootstrap contract before creating the first frontend code.
Scaffold frontend routes, components, and feature folders per the agreed structure.
Use the expected component and composition patterns for the first UI pass.
Shape upload, auth, and data hooks consistently.
Use Zustand, localStorage, and IndexedDB according to the documented boundaries.
Keep API types and runtime boundaries typed consistently.
Follow frontend quality and testing expectations while scaffolding the app.
The implementation will create many new modules; reuse and single-source rules should be explicit from the start.
This task spans storage, API, auth, persistence, and frontend flows across multiple layers.
Remember the repository started empty and treat bootstrap specs as the first-pass contract.
